subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyse the story 'The Cherry Tree'
Interpret the symbolism of the cherry tree and its growth
Evaluate the relationship between Rakesh and his grandfather
Understand how resilience and patience are portrayed in the story
Identify narrative structure and literary elements used by the author
Develop analytical reading, vocabulary, and reflective writing skills

lesson_plan.title: Context and Setting of the Story
topics_covered: Introduction to Ruskin Bond and his writing style
teaching_strategy: Reading and analysing the opening paragraphs
student_activities: Describe the setting of Mussoorie and its environment
Discuss how nature shapes the story’s atmosphere

lesson_plan.title: Character Analysis
topics_covered: Role of the grandfather as a mentor
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of character traits and motivations
student_activities: List the qualities of Rakesh and his grandfather
Write a short paragraph analysing their relationship

lesson_plan.title: Growth and Symbolism of the Cherry Tree
topics_covered: Stages of the cherry tree’s growth
Parallel growth of Rakesh and the tree
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of symbolism in literature
Discussion about how the tree represents growth and perseverance
student_activities: Create a growth timeline of the cherry tree
Explain how the tree symbolises Rakesh’s development

lesson_plan.title: Conflict and Resilience
topics_covered: Challenges faced by the cherry tree
Tree being cut by a woman
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of resilience in nature
student_activities: Identify major conflicts in the story
Discuss how the tree survives each difficulty

lesson_plan.title: Climax and Philosophical Reflection
topics_covered: Rakesh’s emotional connection with the tree
teaching_strategy: Close reading of the concluding paragraphs
student_activities: Interpret the line where Rakesh wonders about being a creator
Write a reflective paragraph on the ending

lesson_plan.title: Literary Analysis and Language Practice
student_activities: Identify introduction, conflict, climax, and resolution
Participate in a short revision quiz

teaching_aids: Pictures or videos of cherry trees
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyse the symbolic meaning of the cherry tree and relate it to personal growth and perseverance. This will deepen their understanding of the story’s message.
